name: Restore the accepted-correct control flow and switch the 64x64 column sweep to explicit right-left-right-left order

summary.name: Restore the accepted-correct control flow and switch the 64x64 column sweep to explicit right-left-right-left order
summary.hypothesis: Three rounds proved that the peeled steady-state control idea is fast but not quickly repairable. The next high-signal move is to separate correctness from warp scheduling again: restore the proven generic hot-band loop, keep the shared PTX hot-band family, and apply the user's warp-local register-reuse idea directly by changing the 64x64 B-column sweep from the current left-right mirrored order to an explicit right-left-right-left order. That gives one clean experiment on the accepted-correct surface and tells us whether warp-local operand order is the real remaining headroom.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Per-warp operand delivery and register reuse inside the 64x64 PTX hot-band microkernel rather than CTA-level pipeline control.
summary.risk: Moderate. The control-flow restore is straightforward, and the new behavior is isolated to warp-local fragment order on the proven correct surface.
summary.stop_condition: Reject if the restored control flow is correct but the right-left-right-left sweep leaves hot-band time flat or regresses relative to the accepted-correct surface.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
